title: Kestrel Scale Search Solutions
preview:
Account summary: Kestrel is a B2B search platform used by several Fortune 500 clients. Moving from a custom ES+FAISS stack to an opinionated vendor to reduce ops burden. Primary drivers: unit cost per query at scale, predictable tail latency for reranking flows, and compliance/residency needs.

Key technical posture...
text:
Account summary: Kestrel is a B2B search platform used by several Fortune 500 clients. Moving from a custom ES+FAISS stack to an opinionated vendor to reduce ops burden. Primary drivers: unit cost per query at scale, predictable tail latency for reranking flows, and compliance/residency needs.

Key technical posture and asks: they plan to index 400M-600M dense vectors initially, target 1B+ within 18 months. Workloads are heavy on embeddings + reranking: candidate recall from embeddings then 3-stage reranker cascade (cheap model for coarse rerank + mid-tier reranker + final expensive ensemble). Want autoscaling for spikes (daily peak windows), persistent KV cache for long sessions, and deterministic batching controls to hit cost SLOs.

Quotes / SDR notes: "We need <150ms P90 for first-pass retrieval and <900ms end-to-end rerank for 99% of queries" — Head of Search.
"If Redwood can show...

text: Need hosted API pilot that demonstrates: 1) streaming chat with function-calling to Stripe/Zuora without exposing card data; 2) strict validated JSON outputs for downstream processing; 3) latency SLOs: 90th pct first-byte <=300ms, typical completions <=2s; 4) audit logs + KMS; 5) SAML SSO & SCIM for provisioning. If hosted pilot meets latency/cost targets, evaluate Dedicated for predictable throughput and tighter VPC control.

text: Early-stage SaaS (B2B marketplace search). Founder Maya Lopez very technical (ex-ML engineer) — implemented quick PoC using SDK, hit token limit. Primary need: embeddings for semantic doc search + short-form summarization for agent responses. Latency target: <200ms p50 for text-gen on small prompts, embeddings throughput 50 qps for batch indexing. Cost sensitivity high; wants clear per-token estimate. Prefers public cloud self-serve flow; comfortable with Stripe card payment but procurement wants option to invoice once we cross $2k/month. Asked specifically: 'can you extend credits for our team for a 2-week POC? invoice option? any startup discount?' SE noted potential to offer 30d credit extension + 20% startup promo. text:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics) evaluating Redwood Hosted API first for rapid POC then a move to Dedicated for stable throughput. Primary workload = embeddings + semantic search across telemetry, product docs, and customer notes. 

Customer pains/requirements (verbatim & paraphrase):
- "We need nightly batch ingest of 200k new vectors and realtime updates for ~2k hot docs/day."
- Must keep vector payloads in regional S3 and ensure embeddings never leave customer VPC for Dedicated option.
- Latency target: search+rerank (3-5 top hits) end-to-end <= 120ms for interactive UI, background report jobs can tolerate higher latency.
- Throughput target: 2k qps during peak product telemetry queries; realistic steady-state ~150 qps. Batch ingest windows ~2-4 hours nightly.
- Cost sensitivity: looking to reduce per-vector cost vs current OpenAI spend; interested in quantization and b...

title: Nimbus Knowledge Labs
preview: Summary: Nimbus Knowledge Labs is a rapidly growing SaaS helpdesk platform (B2B) that wants to replace current keyword/full-text search with a production RAG pipeline: vector embeddings + lightweight reranker for top-k answer precision. They are specifically focused on high QPS (peak ~1k-1.5k qps), moderate index co...
text:
Summary: Nimbus Knowledge Labs is a rapidly growing SaaS helpdesk platform (B2B) that wants to replace current keyword/full-text search with a production RAG pipeline: vector embeddings + lightweight reranker for top-k answer precision. They are specifically focused on high QPS (peak ~1k-1.5k qps), moderate index corpus (1–3M canonical docs, ~40–70GB embeddings), and tight median latency targets (median end-to-end <200ms for interactive agent lookups).

Workload details / requirements: 
- Ingest cadence: nightly delta + real-time small doc adds (~500/day).
- Index scale target: initial 1M docs, plan to scale to 3M within 12 months. Estimated embedding storage ~50GB (float16) before compression/quantization.
- Query profile: 70% short agent queries (1–3 sentences), 30% long contextual retrievals. Expected P95 latency budget for retrieval+rerank: <350ms; median <200ms.
- Throughput: con...

SLA negotiation highlights:

text:
Account snapshot: global market-data provider; latency-sensitive trading assist and real-time enrichment. Key asks centered on ironclad SLA (uptime + credits), deterministic latency, reserved GPU planning and predictable maintenance windows.

SLA negotiation highlights:
- Customer requests: 99.99% uptime for primary trading endpoints, 99.95% for non-critical APIs. Wants credits to scale and not be de minimis.
- Redwood baseline: proposed 99.95% platform SLA for Dedicated; offered add-on for 99.99% with increased reserved capacity and dedicated engineer.
- Credit schedule discussed (customer pushed for formula). Current proposed sliding scale (example):
 * uptime >= 99.95%: 0% credits
 * 99.9% <= uptime < 99.95%: 10% of monthly Dedicated fee
 * 99.0% <= uptime < 99.9%: 30% credit
 * uptime < 99.0%: 100% credit (customer insisted on cap at 100% monthly fee)
- Exclusions: scheduled maint...

text:
POC scope: agent-assist + ticket summarization + tool-calling integration with ServiceNow. Requirements summary:
- RAG with multi-source retrieval: Zendesk articles, Confluence, S3 attachments, older ticket corpus.
- Strong citation requirement: agents must see source links and confidence score.
- Freshness: nightly snapshot + on-demand reindex for edited articles; legal wants 24h SLA for new content to be searchable.
- Reliability: need predictable latency during spikes (Black Friday-like loads). Interested in dedicated pools or private deployment for latency guarantees.
- Cost sensitivity: large seasonal spikes — wants autoscaling with predictable billing and a fallback cheaper model for low-priority queries.
- Tool-calling: must be able to open/append ServiceNow tickets and suggest macros; audit trail required.

text:
Overview: Arcadia Promptcraft builds a VS Code extension that provides streaming completions, inline suggestions and fuzzy signature help for JS/TS + Python. Team is engineering-first; prototype shipped but performance issues at scale.

Primary ask: low-latency streaming + per-extension per-user rate limits. They want median inline suggestion RTT <80ms for local dev workflows and tail P99 <200ms for short completions (<64 tokens). Want to support model/version pinning per-channel so customers can choose a lightweight quantized variant for IDE while defaulting to higher-quality model on demand.

Security: Must integrate with customer SSO (SAML), audit logs for suggestion events, and KMS integration for secrets. Data residency not strict but they want VPC option for enterprise customers.

Metrics/POC success criteria: 1) median suggestion latency <80ms; 2) acceptance rate uplift >25% vs...

title: HexaPatch Labs
preview:
Account background: HexaPatch Labs builds an IDE-integrated assistant focused on GitHub-style PR review workflows. Small engineering org (approx 120 EE) focused on developer velocity for mid-market SaaS. They ship a VS Code extension + server side review service.

Key asks / requirements:

text:
Account background: HexaPatch Labs builds an IDE-integrated assistant focused on GitHub-style PR review workflows. Small engineering org (approx 120 EE) focused on developer velocity for mid-market SaaS. They ship a VS Code extension + server side review service.

Key asks / requirements:
- Ultra-low token latency for streaming diff summaries and inline comment generation (goal: <150ms token latency for short responses, <300ms end-to-end for a 6-10 line diff).
- Support for model/version pinning per-customer and per-workspace; rollback on bad generation.
- Deterministic reranking API for suggestions (stable scores across runs).
- Efficient KV/prefix caching so repeated file-context compiles cheaply (hot files across teams).
- Cost-sensitivity: wants batching and cache-driven downsampling to control token spend for heavy PR traffic.
- Security: must support SSO (SAML), audit logs, KMS ...

text:
Summary:
- Deepwell runs a 20M-document media archive (news articles, transcripts, image captions). avg doc length ~5k words; many very long transcripts.
- Primary need: fast, high-quality search for editors and legal teams (latency target: <300ms to first relevant for UX; 95th <800ms including reranker).
- Cost constraint: want cost/query ~ <$0.02 overall for typical sessions (initial target $0.008 - $0.015 per query), high sensitivity to per-token + reranker GPU time.

Technical / POC plan (from AE/SE call):
- Retrieval stack: dense bi-encoder for candidate retrieval (FAISS) + GPU cross-encoder reranker for top-K. K in discussion (50-200).
- Indexing scale: expect ~80M chunks after chunking (current estimate). Concerned about index sharding, RAM footprint, and search tail latency.
- Chunking experiments: compare 512-token chunks vs 1024-token chunks + 20% overlap. Tradeoffs discusse...

Initial discovery: product team wants to reduce handle time f...
text:
POC focused on automated ticket and email summarization + auto-tagging for Zendesk and Freshdesk workflows. Primary ask: generate 2–3 line TL;DR summaries suitable for agents and macros, and deterministic tag suggestions (intent, product area, SLA tier).

Initial discovery: product team wants to reduce handle time for Tier 1 by 30% and improve triage accuracy for routing to specialists. Current volume: 25k tickets/month, peak bursts 2–3x during promotions. They run Zendesk with custom macros; Freshdesk on a few pilot lines. Data retention policy: 2 years, some EU-origin data must stay in EU region.

Technical constraints: must integrate with Zendesk app framework (app needs to pass OAuth and run within agent UI), support webhook-based async summarization for long threads, and offer deterministic tag outputs to feed into existing triggers/macros. Important: fallback behavior during spi...

text: Mid-market product analytics SaaS (in-app search + chat for B2B customers). Began with Hosted API POC to validate accuracy and UX; moved to full evaluation after cost/latency modeling showed hosted costs could be high at scale. Highly interested in Dedicated to get predictable unit economics and VPC isolation. Strong emphasis on cost engineering: batching, caching, quantization profiles. Redwood ran a hands-on cost-optimization workshop with engineering + product. SE engaged; procurement & security loop started.

title: Fabrikam
preview: Fabrikam is an existing Hosted API customer up for renewal in March. They anticipate a step-function increase in LLM usage due to a Q2 rollout of an AI agent inside their support product. Main renewal theme: shift from reactive pay-as-you-go to a predictable annual commit with guardrails (clear usage definitions, in...
text: Fabrikam is an existing Hosted API customer up for renewal in March. They anticipate a step-function increase in LLM usage due to a Q2 rollout of an AI agent inside their support product. Main renewal theme: shift from reactive pay-as-you-go to a predictable annual commit with guardrails (clear usage definitions, invoice preview, and alerts for spend/run-rate). They are receptive to adding Dedicated baseline capacity for steady traffic but want the option to burst to Hosted API without surprise bills.

title: Northwind Labs
preview: Workload: RAG-based support agent + doc search (embeddings) + summarization jobs. Latency target: p95 < 800ms for chat (short prompts), embeddings can be batch/async. Throughput: bursts during business hours; wants guidance on caching/batching to manage cost. Model needs: prefers a stable default model with fallback...
text: Workload: RAG-based support agent + doc search (embeddings) + summarization jobs. Latency target: p95 < 800ms for chat (short prompts), embeddings can be batch/async. Throughput: bursts during business hours; wants guidance on caching/batching to manage cost. Model needs: prefers a stable default model with fallback option (customer said: "we can accept a slightly smaller fallback model if it keeps us online"). Metering/reporting: strong emphasis on finance-grade telemetry—daily usage export in UTC, by model + region, with separate prompt/completion/billed token fields; wants cache-hit attribution shown and ideally excluded from billed usage if policy is to not charge cached tokens (they will accept either policy but want it explicit). Retries/streaming semantics: needs clarity on whether partial streams count, and how server-side retries/fallback affect billed tokens (concern about d...

title: Calypso ServeFlow
preview:
Summary: Calypso ServeFlow is a fintech payments orchestration platform that wants to embed real-time conversational and recommendation features into their merchant dashboard. Very latency- and cost-sensitive — they price UX per-transaction and need predictable unit economics.

Key constraints / asks (from CTO + inf...
text:
Summary: Calypso ServeFlow is a fintech payments orchestration platform that wants to embed real-time conversational and recommendation features into their merchant dashboard. Very latency- and cost-sensitive — they price UX per-transaction and need predictable unit economics.

Key constraints / asks (from CTO + infra lead):
- SLO targets: median latency <= 35-50ms for 128-256 token chat paths; p95 < 75-90ms.
- Throughput: baseline 200 qps with bursts to 1k qps during peak settlement windows.
- Cost sensitivity: target incremental cost <= $0.50 per 1000 messages (after caching/batching).
- Model preferences: open Llama-style models for deterministic licensing; willing to evaluate small proprietary variants if latency improves.
- Routing/fallback: need policy for model fallback to lower-cost quantized variants during capacity pressure.
- Security: VPC + customer-managed KMS, audit logs...

title: Cobalt Orbit Inference
preview:
Background:
- Mid-size logistics platform, operates real-time route optimization + driver assistant chat. High-read consistency for embeddings + sub-100ms soft SLO for short chat turns on edge routes.
- Interested in Redwood Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation; on-prem option for EU-sensitive cus...
text:
Background:
- Mid-size logistics platform, operates real-time route optimization + driver assistant chat. High-read consistency for embeddings + sub-100ms soft SLO for short chat turns on edge routes.
- Interested in Redwood Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation; on-prem option for EU-sensitive customers.

Primary objectives (from AE/SE discussions):
- Throughput guarantees for business hours regional peaks: target 12k RPS of small-chat tokens across 3 regions (NA/EU/APAC) with 99th percentile latency SLOs: 50-120ms depending on model variant.
- Predictable reserved GPU planning: want recommendation for reserved vs burst mix (monthly commit + burst on demand). Need example ARRs for 4x/6x utilization scenarios.
- Reliability: cross-region failover with <5s cold-swap for routing + model-variant fallback when primary variants saturate. Documented sequence for model fall...

text: Enterprise financial services account in procurement. Strong interest in Redwood Private (VPC) + Dedicated capacity. Metering constraints will impact what we can show in Console and what Finance can access for recon.

Primary contact: Mara Lin (Head of Product). Key asks: 1) SOC2 Type 2 evidence summary (not full report) 2) Sub-processors list (who processes logs, metrics, m...
text:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve signup. Started trial 2026-02-10, small volume (a few thousand tokens/day) but high sensitivity around customer PII handling. Primary contact: Mara Lin (Head of Product). Key asks: 1) SOC2 Type 2 evidence summary (not full report) 2) Sub-processors list (who processes logs, metrics, model infra) 3) DPA with standard EU clauses even though HQ in NA — wants DPA to be ready for legal review. They use AWS us-east-1; prefer public cloud hosted API. Cost sensitivity: high, want to keep monthly spend < $2k while scaling tests. "Mara: we love the latency numbers but need paperwork before ramp".
- Trial usage shows chat + doc-search combo, mainly conversational support autopilot and embedding-based KB.
- Security/Legal pinged us 2026-02-16 with a 12-question mini-questionnaire (attached). We answered 6/12, asked for SOC2 summary and subprocessors list for remain...

title: HatchLab Copilot
preview:
Summary: HatchLab builds an IDE copilot focused on test-run orchestration and live build logs. They want function-calling so the assistant can invoke test runners, linters, and a sandboxed build agent from inside the IDE and stream logs back in <200ms for interactive UX.

Key requirements / constraints:
- Sandboxed ...
text:
Summary: HatchLab builds an IDE copilot focused on test-run orchestration and live build logs. They want function-calling so the assistant can invoke test runners, linters, and a sandboxed build agent from inside the IDE and stream logs back in <200ms for interactive UX.

Key requirements / constraints:
- Sandboxed function-calls: must run in hermetic containers with no outbound access, ephemeral FS, and resource limits. Customer insists on 'no internet egress' from build agents.
- Low-latency streaming: in-IDE tail of build logs must be near-real-time (target <200ms tail-to-client for small builds; acceptable up to 500ms for larger CI jobs).
- Model/version pinning: want deterministic assistants tied to specific model versions (pinning for reproducible behavior during test runs); ability to rollback to older quantized model if regression detected.
- Tooling surface: function call API...

text:
- SMB ecommerce platform building AI-powered product search and conversational support.
- Primary objective: replace current embed/rerank pipeline (Algolia + custom ranking) to reduce infra ops.
- Volume profile: P0: chat-based support (bursty, low latency), P1: embeddings for search (batch hourly), estimated ~150-250k tokens/month initially.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — early stage, expects self-serve pricing to be competitive; will expand if unit economics hold.
- Security ask: 'Can you provide SOC2 Type II and list of sub-processors? Our GC needs DPA before billing/account upgrade.' — paraphrase from Rajesh.
- Data residency: majority NA, but ~8% EU customers; legal wants confirmation on whether EU data can be pinned to eu-1 region or if public-cloud multi-region is used.
- Preferred models: open-source fine if hosted by Redwood; want a small-latency model for chat (<=200ms p95) ...

text:
Lead: Jordan Blake (AE). SE: Priya Menon. CSM: Alex Ruiz assigned for transition planning.

Discovery summary:
- 2025-10-07: Intro call; Aquifer uses LLMs for vehicle incident triage + operator chat. Interested in dedicated GPU pools for deterministic latency.
- 2025-11-12: Shared annotated prompt set (2.1k examples) and initial traffic profile (peak 600 rps for embeddings; 120 rps chat during surge).
- 2025-12-02: Security questionnaire submitted; SOC2 + KMS required. Data residency: US only for PII subsets.
- 2026-01-18: Architecture workshop with SE team; discussed KV-cache, batching, and multi-model routing for cost control.
- 2026-02-10: POC kickoff call (Fireflies id ff_20260210_aurora-poc-call). Agreed test plan and metrics.

POC goals (what Aquifer needs):
- Validate sustained throughput on Dedicated pool: 3k tokens/sec sustained with 99% of requests meeting latency SLO.
- Tai...

title: Acme Health
preview: Primary workload: (1) clinician-facing summarization of long-form notes (streaming preferred), (2) internal doc-search/RAG over policies/procedures, (3) support-agent style Q&A for staff. Throughput: initial ~50–150 concurrent users, spiky during business hours; latency goal stated as “<2s time-to-first-token for ty...
text:
Primary workload: (1) clinician-facing summarization of long-form notes (streaming preferred), (2) internal doc-search/RAG over policies/procedures, (3) support-agent style Q&A for staff. Throughput: initial ~50–150 concurrent users, spiky during business hours; latency goal stated as “<2s time-to-first-token for typical prompts” for the summarization UI. Strong cost sensitivity for long-context summaries; asked about batching/caching and model/quantization options.

Deployment preference: Private (VPC) strongly preferred; Dedicated is a fallback only if we can document strict isolation, retention controls, and clear shared responsibility. They explicitly do not want prompts/responses retained beyond what is necessary for operations.

Security/compliance: SOC 2 Type II report required under NDA. Asked multiple times about HIPAA/BAA: want a vendor willing to sign a BAA for any workflow...
links: (none recorded in CRM; most comms in buyer portal + Slack channel per SE)

title: Axiom Bridge AI

text: Large fintech using LLMs for realtime support and transaction summarization. Engaged for evaluation of hosted API vs dedicated GPU pool. Initial POC showed quality OK but projected per-token and GPU run costs exceed their internal target; procurement froze. Primary loss vector: unit economics (token/GPU cost + commit size) do not meet finance constraints.

title: Ironbrook DataWorks
preview: Summary: Mid-market B2B product analytics company. Started with Redwood Hosted API during Dec trial to validate embeddings + chat for their in-app support assistant and a reranking pipeline for search relevance. Trial showed good quality but cost and latency variability during spikes triggered evaluation for Dedicat...
text:
Summary: Mid-market B2B product analytics company. Started with Redwood Hosted API during Dec trial to validate embeddings + chat for their in-app support assistant and a reranking pipeline for search relevance. Trial showed good quality but cost and latency variability during spikes triggered evaluation for Dedicated/reserved capacity.

Trial metrics observed (hosted):
- average QPS during weekday: 4 qps (observed 2026-01-04 to 2026-01-31)
- peak QPS observed during product release: 12 qps (single 3-hour burst)
- observed typical concurrency: 6 concurrent streams, peaks to 28 concurrent streams during load tests
- token mix: prompts ~350 tokens on average, completions ~150 tokens average; embeddings calls ~512 vector dims, 45% of calls are embeddings, 35% chat/generation, 20% reranking/short prompts
- P50 latency (hosted): 90ms for embeddings, 220ms for chat when KV cache hit; P95 sp...

text:
HubSpot Company Record — Devflow AI (devflow.ai)

Summary
- Mid-market devtools team building an LLM-powered coding assistant inside their IDE + web app.
- Came in via self-serve Hosted API; now in evaluation with potential Dedicated capacity request if p95 latency holds at launch traffic.
- Strong sensitivity to onboarding friction (they’re modeling their own user onboarding after ours).

Lifecycle touchpoints (sprint instrumentation)
- Onboarding Email v2: received (Variant B; subject emphasized “fast first request”).
 - Observed behavior: clicked CTA → quickstart page → returned to console within ~6 minutes.
 - Feedback: “The email got us to the right page, but the snippet didn’t match our setup (we’re TS/Node + Vite).”
- Console nudges:
 - Nudge shown: Create API key (completed).
 - Nudge shown: Make your first request (clicked; led to docs). Dismissal persisted correctly after co...
